_note: Curated map of the E4L non-infoceutical ENVIRONMENTAL stress items -> target organs/systems + detox-support remedies, for the clinical glossary. Sourced from a 2026-07-14 toxicology research pass. HONESTY: most of these are cleared by normal liver Phase-II conjugation (glutathione/GST/UGT) + renal/biliary excretion, NOT by a specific product; only PFAS (bile-acid sequestrants) and fluoride (Ca/Mg gut binding) have a documented binder, and asbestos/isocyanates have no metabolic detox (avoidance only). Remedies below are framed as general elimination/conjugation support, not cures. Tiers: strong (agency/IARC) / moderate / weak. `affects` names cross-link to clinical Organ entries where they match.

dimensions.title: Environmental Stressors
dimensions.blurb: Heavy metals and everyday chemical exposures an E4L scan can flag — their target organs and the elimination pathways that clear them.

entries.name: Aluminum
entries.description: Toxic mainly when the gut barrier is bypassed and renal excretion is impaired. Dialysis encephalopathy and bone osteomalacia are established; the Alzheimer's link is contested. Cleared renally; binds gut phosphate.

remedies.name: ES15 Heavy Metals Energetic Star Infoceutical
Chelation
Binder Complex
Glutathione Syntropy

entries.name: Arsenic
entries.description: IARC Group 1. Skin keratosis is the sentinel sign; also vascular (blackfoot disease, atherosclerosis), bladder and lung. Cleared by methylation + renal excretion.

remedies.name: ES15 Heavy Metals Energetic Star Infoceutical
Chelation
Binder Complex
Glutathione Syntropy

entries.name: Cadmium
entries.description: Primary target is the kidney proximal tubule (metallothionein-bound, endocytosed via megalin:cubilin); also bone (itai-itai) and lung. Very long half-life.

remedies.name: ES15 Heavy Metals Energetic Star Infoceutical
Chelation
Binder Complex
Glutathione Syntropy

entries.name: Lead
entries.description: Bone is the reservoir (~94% of body burden, remobilizes in pregnancy/menopause); the CNS is the critical target; also kidney and heme (anemia).

remedies.name: ES15 Heavy Metals Energetic Star Infoceutical
Chelation
Binder Complex
Glutathione Syntropy

entries.name: Bisphenol A (BPA)
entries.description: Estrogen-receptor agonist (endocrine disruptor); alters reproductive/mammary/ovarian signaling and metabolism. Short half-life (~6 h); cleared by liver Phase-II glucuronidation → urine.

remedies.name: Liver Support
Glutathione Syntropy
DTX-LIVER Homeopathic Complex in Terrain Restore

entries.name: Phthalates
entries.description: Anti-androgenic ('phthalate syndrome': ↓fetal testosterone → hypospadias, cryptorchidism); rodent hepatic PPARα effects. Rapidly hydrolyzed + glucuronidated → urine/feces.

remedies.name: Liver Support
Glutathione Syntropy

entries.name: Parabens
entries.description: Weakly estrogenic; mainly a skin/mucosal irritant-sensitizer. Rapidly hydrolyzed by esterases (>70% renal in 24 h) — organ-specific systemic toxicology is thin.

remedies.name: Liver Support

entries.name: Flame Retardants
entries.description: PBDEs disrupt the thyroid (HPT axis) + liver and the developing brain; lipophilic, bioaccumulative, long half-life. Hydroxylated by CYP450 → glucuronide/sulfate → biliary/fecal.

remedies.name: Binder Complex
Fiber Cleanse
Glutathione Syntropy
Liver Support

entries.name: Teflon / PFAS (PTFE)
entries.description: PFOA/PFOS are persistent and NOT metabolized (half-life ~5 yr) — liver accumulation, immune suppression, cholesterol, thyroid. Documented binder: bile-acid sequestrants interrupt enterohepatic recirculation (~38% serum drop).

remedies.name: Binder Complex
Fiber Cleanse
Glutathione Syntropy
Liver Support

entries.name: Formaldehyde
entries.description: IARC Group 1. Reactive electrophile → DNA-protein crosslinks at the point of contact (nasal/respiratory epithelium, eyes/skin); myeloid leukemia debated. Very short half-life via GSH-dependent ADH5 → formate.

remedies.name: Glutathione Syntropy
S-Acetyl Glutathione
Liver Support

entries.name: Azo Dyes
entries.description: Gut/hepatic azoreductase splits the azo bond → aromatic amines. Benzidine-class dyes are IARC Group 1 bladder carcinogens; common FOOD azo colorants (tartrazine, Allura Red) do NOT share that mechanism — don't treat all as one carcinogenic block.

remedies.name: Liver Support
Glutathione Syntropy
Fiber Cleanse

entries.name: Butanols
entries.description: n-Butanol is an ethanol-like CNS depressant and an eye/skin/respiratory irritant. Cleared within hours (ADH→ALDH, mostly exhaled). Little organ-specific systemic toxicity — mainly avoidance/ventilation.

entries.name: Carbonyls
entries.description: Reactive aldehydes (acrolein, acetaldehyde) deplete glutathione and form protein/DNA adducts — respiratory tract, eyes, and (acetaldehyde) upper GI/liver. GSH conjugation → mercapturic acids → urine is central.

remedies.name: Glutathione Syntropy
S-Acetyl Glutathione
Acetaldehyde Detox

entries.name: 1,4-Dioxane
entries.description: IARC 2B. CYP2E1-metabolized; the parent accumulates at saturating doses → hepatic redox dysregulation (liver primary; kidney, nasal). Clearance is saturable/self-inducing.

remedies.name: Liver Support
Glutathione Syntropy

entries.name: Hydroxytoluene (BHT)
entries.description: Activated to a quinone-methide/hydroperoxide → ROS, centrilobular liver damage and tumor promotion, especially under glutathione depletion (mostly rodent data). Hepatic oxidation → glucuronide → urine.

remedies.name: Glutathione Syntropy
Liver Support

entries.name: Isocyanate
entries.description: Reactive N=C=O haptenates self-proteins → sensitization and occupational asthma; direct airway/skin irritant. Harm is a local immune reaction, not a circulating toxin cleared by the liver — prevention is avoidance.

entries.name: N-heptane
entries.description: Lipophilic solvent → nonspecific CNS narcosis and respiratory/skin irritation (weakly neurotoxic vs n-hexane). CYP450 → heptanols → glucuronide/sulfate, renal + pulmonary exhalation.

remedies.name: Glutathione Syntropy
Liver Support

entries.name: Nitrotoluene
entries.description: CYP oxidation + nitroreduction → methemoglobinemia/anemia (blood) and liver; the ortho-isomer is a rodent liver carcinogen (IARC 2A). Human/meta-para data are thin.

remedies.name: Liver Support
Glutathione Syntropy
Blood Cleanse

entries.name: p-Phenylenediamine (PPD)
entries.description: Oxidizes to Bandrowski's base → strong skin (type-IV) allergen; systemic poisoning (from ingestion) → rhabdomyolysis, myoglobinuric kidney injury, angioedema. IARC Group 3. Mostly renal clearance; no antidote.

remedies.name: Liver Support
Glutathione Syntropy

entries.name: Petrochemicals
entries.description: Volatile aromatics (benzene/toluene/xylene): acute CNS depression; benzene → bone marrow/hematopoietic (AML, IARC 1); toluene/xylene → liver, kidney. Cleared by CYP2E1 + Phase-II conjugation (GSH/glycine) → urine.

remedies.name: Glutathione Syntropy
Liver Support
Blood Cleanse

entries.name: Smoke
entries.description: PAHs (cigarette/wood smoke) are pro-carcinogens bioactivated by CYP1A1/1B1 → diol-epoxide DNA adducts — lung primary, also skin, bladder, GI. Cleared by GST conjugation + glucuronidation → bile/feces + urine.

remedies.name: Glutathione Syntropy
Liver Support
Oxygen Cleanse

entries.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulphate (SLS)
entries.description: Anionic surfactant that denatures barrier proteins → irritant contact dermatitis (skin/eyes/mucosa); not a true sensitizer. Poorly absorbed; rinsing removes it — no systemic detox needed.

entries.name: Asbestos
entries.description: IARC Group 1. Biopersistent fiber → frustrated phagocytosis, chronic ROS/inflammation, fibrosis (lung parenchyma, pleura/peritoneum). NOT metabolized — removed only physically; durable fibers are retained for years. No binder or metabolic detox exists; the intervention is avoidance.

entries.name: Fluoride
entries.description: Substitutes into hydroxyapatite → skeletal/dental fluorosis; high-dose kidney and acute GI. Skeletal sequestration + renal excretion (falls with kidney impairment). Oral calcium/magnesium bind fluoride in the gut and reduce absorption.

remedies.name: Binder Complex

entries.name: Furnishings
entries.description: An off-gassing composite (formaldehyde, flame retardants, VOCs, isocyanates from foams/finishes) rather than a single agent — see those entries for organ targets. Reduce exposure and support liver Phase-II conjugation.

remedies.name: Glutathione Syntropy
Liver Support

dimensions.title: Nutrition
dimensions.blurb: The nutrient fields an E4L scan can flag as stressed — a signal of need/deficiency — with what each supports and its supplement form.

entries.name: Boron
entries.description: Modulates calcium/magnesium metabolism and steroid-hormone (estrogen, testosterone) + vitamin-D activity. Not classed essential for humans; low status impairs bone metabolism and mental alertness.

entries.name: Calcium
entries.description: The structural mineral of bone; also signals muscle contraction, nerve transmission and clotting. Deficiency → low bone mass, osteoporosis/osteomalacia, tetany.

entries.name: Chromium
entries.description: Enhances insulin action and glucose uptake. Poor status → impaired glucose tolerance / insulin resistance (frank deficiency rare).

entries.name: Cobalt
entries.description: Its only biological role is as the central atom of vitamin B12 (cobalamin) — a cobalt 'stress' reads as B12 status (megaloblastic anemia, neuropathy).

remedies.name: Sublingual B12

entries.name: Copper
entries.description: Cofactor for energy production, iron metabolism, connective-tissue crosslinking and antioxidant defense. Deficiency → anemia, neutropenia, neurological signs.

entries.name: Iodine
entries.description: The essential component of thyroid hormones (T3/T4). Deficiency → goiter, hypothyroidism, and impaired fetal neurodevelopment.

entries.name: Iron
entries.description: Oxygen transport (hemoglobin/myoglobin), electron transport, DNA synthesis. Deficiency → anemia, fatigue, pallor, impaired immunity and cognition.

remedies.name: Iron Syntropy

entries.name: Magnesium
entries.description: Cofactor for 300+ enzymes; ATP, DNA/protein synthesis, a natural calcium-channel antagonist. Deficiency → cramps, arrhythmia, fatigue, neuromuscular hyperexcitability.

remedies.name: Magnesium Synergy

entries.name: Manganese
entries.description: Cofactor for the antioxidant Mn-SOD, bone formation, and carb/amino-acid metabolism. Deficiency rare in humans.

entries.name: Molybdenum
entries.description: Cofactor for sulfite oxidase, xanthine oxidase and aldehyde oxidase (sulfur and purine metabolism / detox). Deficiency extremely rare.

remedies.name: Molybdenum Synergy

entries.name: Phosphorus
entries.description: Bone mineral (with calcium), ATP energy, phospholipid membranes and nucleic acids. Deficiency uncommon; imbalance/excess is the usual concern.

entries.name: Potassium
entries.description: The principal intracellular cation — membrane potential, nerve/muscle function, blood-pressure regulation. Low status → weakness, cramps, arrhythmia, higher BP/stroke risk.

remedies.name: Potassium Citrate

entries.name: Selenium
entries.description: Component of glutathione peroxidase and thyroid deiodinases — antioxidant, thyroid and immune support. Deficiency → Keshan cardiomyopathy, impaired immunity/thyroid.

entries.name: Silica
entries.description: As orthosilicic acid, supports collagen/glycosaminoglycan synthesis and bone-matrix mineralization (skin, hair, nails, connective tissue). No defined human deficiency syndrome.

entries.name: Sodium
entries.description: The principal extracellular cation — fluid balance, blood pressure, nerve/muscle function. Dietary deficiency rare; excess is the public-health issue.

entries.name: Zinc
entries.description: Catalytic/structural cofactor for 300+ enzymes — immune function, wound healing, DNA synthesis, taste/smell. Deficiency → poor immunity/healing, hair loss, dermatitis, taste loss.

remedies.name: Zinc Synergy

entries.name: Cholesterol
entries.description: A structural component of cell membranes and precursor to steroid hormones, vitamin D and bile acids. Endogenously made — a scan 'stress' signals DYSREGULATION (oxidized/LDL imbalance or impaired synthesis), not simple dietary lack.

entries.name: Conjugated Linoleic Acid (CLA)
entries.description: Conjugated isomers of linoleic acid from ruminant fat/dairy; studied for body-composition and immune modulation. Not an essential nutrient — no deficiency state.

entries.name: Omega 3 - ALA
entries.description: The essential parent omega-3; a (limited, <15%) precursor to EPA/DHA and a membrane/energy source. Frank deficiency → scaly dermatitis, neuro/visual signs.

remedies.name: WholOmega 30 gelcaps

entries.name: Omega 3 - DHA
entries.description: The major structural lipid of neural and retinal membranes — critical for brain and vision. Low status → suboptimal cognition/retinal function.

remedies.name: WholOmega 30 gelcaps

entries.name: Omega 3 - EPA
entries.description: Precursor to anti-inflammatory eicosanoids/resolvins — anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular support. Imbalance → pro-inflammatory state, high triglycerides.

remedies.name: WholOmega 30 gelcaps

entries.name: Omega 6 - GLA
entries.description: Omega-6 derived from linoleic acid; precursor to anti-inflammatory series-1 prostaglandins (via DGLA). Useful where delta-6-desaturase conversion is impaired (dry/inflammatory skin).

entries.name: Omega 6 - LA
entries.description: The essential parent omega-6 — membrane structure, skin barrier, precursor to arachidonic acid/eicosanoids. Deficiency (rare) → scaly dermatitis, impaired skin barrier.

entries.name: Omega 9 - OA
entries.description: A non-essential monounsaturated fat (main fat of olive oil) — membrane fluidity and a favorable lipid profile. Body synthesizes it; valued as a saturated-fat replacement.

entries.name: Vitamin A
entries.description: Vision phototransduction, cell differentiation, immune and epithelial/mucosal integrity. Deficiency → night blindness, xerophthalmia, impaired immunity, follicular hyperkeratosis.

remedies.name: Vitamin A Synergy

entries.name: Vitamin B Complex
entries.description: The eight water-soluble B coenzymes driving energy metabolism, methylation and nerve function. Deficiency (varies by member) → fatigue, dermatitis, glossitis, neuropathy, anemia.

remedies.name: Stamina Plus: Full B complex & C HomeoEnergetic Drops

entries.name: Vitamin B1 (Thiamine)
entries.description: TPP coenzyme for carbohydrate metabolism and ATP production. Deficiency → beriberi (cardiac/neuropathic), Wernicke-Korsakoff, fatigue.

entries.name: Vitamin B2 (Riboflavin)
entries.description: Precursor of the FAD/FMN flavin coenzymes for redox and energy metabolism. Deficiency → angular cheilitis/stomatitis, glossitis, photophobia, anemia.

entries.name: Vitamin B3 (Niacin)
entries.description: NAD/NADP coenzymes for energy metabolism, DNA repair and signaling. Deficiency → pellagra (dermatitis, diarrhea, dementia).

entries.name: Vitamin B5 (Pantothenic Acid)
entries.description: Component of coenzyme A and ACP for fatty-acid, energy and adrenal steroid synthesis. Deficiency (rare) → paresthesias, 'burning feet', fatigue.

entries.name: Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine)
entries.description: PLP coenzyme for amino-acid metabolism, neurotransmitter and heme synthesis. Deficiency → microcytic anemia, dermatitis, depression, peripheral neuropathy.

entries.name: Vitamin B7 (Biotin)
entries.description: Carboxylase coenzyme for gluconeogenesis and fatty-acid/amino-acid metabolism. Deficiency → hair loss, periorificial dermatitis, neurologic signs.

entries.name: Vitamin B8 (Inositol)
entries.description: Cell-membrane phospholipid component and second-messenger signaling (metabolic/insulin regulation). NOT a true vitamin — the body synthesizes it from glucose, so there's no classic deficiency.

remedies.name: Inositol

entries.name: Vitamin B9 (Folate)
entries.description: One-carbon/methyl transfer for DNA synthesis and amino-acid metabolism. Deficiency → megaloblastic anemia, neural-tube defects, elevated homocysteine.

entries.name: Vitamin B10 (PABA)
entries.description: A folate building block (in bacteria) and UV-absorbing compound. NOT essential in humans — there is no human deficiency syndrome.

entries.name: Vitamin B12 (Cobalamin)
entries.description: Coenzyme for methylation, DNA synthesis and myelin maintenance. Deficiency → megaloblastic/pernicious anemia, neuropathy, cognitive changes.

remedies.name: Sublingual B12

entries.name: Vitamin C
entries.description: Antioxidant and cofactor for collagen, carnitine and neurotransmitter synthesis. Deficiency → scurvy (bleeding gums, poor healing, bruising, fatigue).

entries.name: Vitamin D
entries.description: Regulates calcium/phosphate homeostasis and bone mineralization; modulates immunity. Deficiency → rickets/osteomalacia, bone pain, muscle weakness.

remedies.name: Vitamin D Synergy

entries.name: Vitamin E
entries.description: The lipid-soluble antioxidant protecting cell membranes. Deficiency → peripheral neuropathy, ataxia, myopathy, hemolytic anemia.

remedies.name: Vitamin E Spectrum

entries.name: Vitamin K
entries.description: Cofactor for gamma-carboxylation of clotting factors and bone proteins. Deficiency → easy bruising/bleeding, prolonged clotting.

entries.name: Choline
entries.description: Precursor of acetylcholine and phospholipids, and a methyl donor (via betaine). A vitamin-like essential nutrient. Deficiency → fatty liver, muscle damage, elevated homocysteine.

entries.name: Betaine / TMG
entries.description: A methyl donor that remethylates homocysteine to methionine, and a cellular osmolyte. Vitamin-like; low levels → elevated homocysteine.

remedies.name: TMG Syntropy Powder
